refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall minimize energy consumption per ton moved to meet or exceed the customer-defined energy efficiency target.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the high-level FR into a measurable and actionable sub-FR focused on energy consumption per ton moved aligns with the acceptance criteria and enables targeted design and verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure total energy used and tons moved over the evaluation period and calculate energy_per_ton to verify it meets ≤ E_target.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor and report energy consumption accurately.
FR2: Optimize vehicle operation to reduce energy per ton moved.
FR3: Provide feedback and control to maintain energy efficiency target.

candidate_dps: DP1: Energy consumption monitoring system with telemetry integration.
DP3: Energy efficiency feedback control module.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Energy consumption monitoring system with telemetry integration.

potential_sources: Telemetry data dependency between monitoring and vehicle operation modules
Feedback control coupling with vehicle powertrain optimization
coupling_check.notes: DP1 logically supports FR1 independently but requires interface coordination with DP2 and DP3 for full system performance.

assumptions_risks: Assumes accurate and timely telemetry data is available from vehicle sensors.
Risk that telemetry data latency or loss could degrade energy monitoring accuracy.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider modularizing telemetry interface to isolate data acquisition risks.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Physical vehicle systems including sensors and powertrain components responsible for energy consumption measurement and optimization.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle sends energy consumption and operational data to NGHE Solution for monitoring and analysis.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives operational commands from Teleoperation or NGHE Solution to adjust vehicle behavior for energy efficiency.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment of energy monitoring and optimization functions within Vehicle; low diffusion as interfaces are well-defined.
Contains system-level monitoring and analysis but less direct control over energy measurement hardware.
Limited containment; mainly issues commands, no direct energy monitoring.
Minimal involvement in energy efficiency functions.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Energy consumption monitoring and optimization are best contained within the Vehicle module to leverage direct sensor access and control over powertrain components.
containment_risks: Potential complexity in integrating telemetry data with NGHE Solution analytics.
Risk of insufficient modular separation if Vehicle module becomes too large or complex.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Evaluate splitting telemetry data acquisition into a dedicated submodule within Vehicle or NGHE Solution to improve modularity.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (energy consumption monitoring system) directly addresses the refined FR and supports measurable verification.

diffusion_risks: Telemetry interface dependency between Vehicle and NGHE Solution could cause data latency or loss.
Potential overlap in control logic between Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment within Vehicle is strong for energy monitoring; interfaces are clearly defined to minimize diffusion.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by measuring energy consumption and tons moved using Vehicle sensors and telemetry data.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of energy monitoring and optimization to Vehicle module with well-defined external interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Consider modularizing telemetry data acquisition to reduce diffusion risks.
Clarify interface protocols between Vehicle and NGHE Solution for telemetry and tasking.

decision.justification: While the decomposition and assignments are sound, telemetry interface diffusion and modularity concerns warrant refinement before proceeding.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor and report energy consumption accurately.
FR2: Optimize vehicle operation to reduce energy per ton moved.
FR3: Provide feedback and control to maintain energy efficiency target.

revise.why: Improve modular separation of telemetry data acquisition and clarify interface definitions to reduce diffusion and integration risks.

assumptions: Telemetry data is reliable and timely.
Vehicle module can contain energy monitoring and optimization functions.
open_risks: Telemetry data latency or loss impacting energy efficiency monitoring accuracy.
Potential complexity in Vehicle module if too many functions are contained.
evidence_needed: Telemetry data quality and latency measurements.
Module containment analysis with refined telemetry submodule.
